observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:
a. Direct the City Development Council (CDC) to adhere to the revised guidelines in the allocation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund pursuant to DBM, DOF and DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020.
b. Ensure that the projects funded out of the 20% Development Fund are specific and itemized pursuant to article 454(d) of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 7160 and discontinue the practice of appropriating funds in lumpsum amounts; and
c. If the PPAs under the 20% DF are in the lumpsum, obtain prior approval or authorization from the Sangguniang Panlungsod before implementing, in accordance with the SC ruling under GR No. 175527.

We recommended that the City Mayor, specifically the Local Development Council:
a. Prioritize specific projects to be funded under the 20% Development Fund as embodied in the City’s duly approved development plan to address the community’s most pressing needs.
b. Ascertain that the projects are necessary, appropriate, or incidental to efficient and effective governance and essential for promotion of the general welfare of the people; and
c. Ensure that the projects to be financed by the DF are well planned, procurement and implementation ready pursuant to DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No.1 dated November 4, 2020.

We recommended and Management agreed to refrain from procuring high-end devices if it is not circumstantially justified.
We also recommended that before initiating the procurement process, the end user or the Bids and Awards Committee conduct thorough market research and price comparison to obtain competitive pricing for similar devices in the market and establish a baseline for evaluating the price quotations offered by the suppliers in order to determine the most advantageous price for the government.
observations.reason: The same practice is still implemented by the City.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the two SCO restitute/remit the missing funds totaling ₱620,929.73, be relieved as collectors, and be filed with appropriate charges, if warranted.
We also recommended that the City Treasurer strictly impose the regulation on the daily turnover of collections intact to him and designate a Liquidating Officer to take charge of all remittances of the city collectors.
We also recommended that the City Treasurer control the issuance of accountable forms (AF) to all city collectors and refrain from issuing AF to collectors that could not remit intact all collections received to avoid similar incidents.
observations.managementAction: The SCO already started paying the amount through salary deduction.
observations.reason: However, one of the SCO was suspended by the Ombudsman. Thus, no salary deduction can be made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Budget Officer refrain from approving expenditures with insufficient appropriation and consider initiating the augmentation of the budget when there are unforeseen expenses or emergencies to ensure adequate funding for critical needs in strict adherence to Section 4 of PD No. 1445 and Section 336 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the City Mayor or the procuring Entity (PE):
a. Direct the end-users to conduct project costing through market research/study of the goods to be procured and consider other cost components as well as project costing and market study in determining the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) which could have ascertained the most reasonable and fair cost of goods; thus, there is no assurance that the goods purchased were obtained at the most advantageous price for the government;
b. The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) widely disseminate the RFQs to suppliers of known qualifications and accredited by PhilGEPS or distribute the invitations of bid to prospective bidders to intensify competition to avail of the most competitive prices of the goods; and
c. Require the Accounting Unit to take charge of the Internal Audit Service (IAS) function by reviewing existing procurement policies and procedures to promote transparency, competition, and fairness in the procurement process. Conduct price benchmarking by comparing the prices paid by the City with prevailing market rates for similar goods and call the attention of the end-user and the BAC if prices are disadvantageous to the city.
observations.reason:
The same practice is still being implemented by the City.
During the audit of procurement of construction materials, we have observed the gather of prices attached to the DVs were supplied by the suppliers.
The Accounting Unit has taken charge to implement verification and internal review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Engineer review and approve the infrastructure projects' POW and IAR following the existing rules and regulations.

We recommended and the City Assessor agreed to periodically update the land valuation and submit an updated appraisal and assessment roll to the City Treasurer as basis for setting the RPT target in the beginning of each year.
We also recommended that the City Treasurer intensify their real property tax collection to meet their targets so that the City can provide additional funds to finance infrastructure and basic services to its constituents.

We recommended that the Management, through the City Budget Officer and the City Accountant, refrain from charging the previous year's obligations against the current year's appropriation and allotment. Likewise, the City Accountant should recognize in the books of accounts the related expenses in the year it was incurred while the previous year's obligations paid in the current year be properly adjusted to the Government Equity account as Prior Period Adjustment.

observations.recommendation:
1.15. We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
• To require the City Assessor to conduct frequent physical surveys
pursuant to Section 472(b)(6) of RA 7160 in order to update the list
of real properties subject to RPT and SET;
• To require the City Accountant, City Treasurer, and City Assessor
to reconcile their records to establish the correct balance of RPT
Receivable and SET Receivable;
• To require the City Accountant to make adjusting entries to correct
the balance of RPT Receivable, SET Receivable and the
corresponding Deferred RPT Income and SET Income; and
• To expedite the adoption of a real property tax system to streamline
the recording and updating of taxable real properties and facilitate
proper accounting, including the maintenance of individual
taxpayer balances.

2.18. We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
• To require the City Accountant and City Engineer to reconcile their
records to identify the completed projects included in the CIP in
order to facilitate the transfer thereof to the appropriate PPE
account/s;
• To require the City Accountant to record the corresponding
depreciation expense, including those pertaining to prior years;
• To require the City Accountant and City Engineer to devise a system
for the monitoring of the completion of infrastructure projects for
the timely reclassification of these projects from CIP to the
appropriate PPE account; and
• To require the City Accountant to investigate the ₱68.654 million
balance within the CIP - Infrastructure Assets account, under Other
Public Infrastructures described as "FOR VERIFICATION", to
determine if it can be written off pursuant to Item 6.3.2(e) of COA
Circular No. 2020-006.

3.8. We initially recommended for the reversal of the prior year (PY) expenses
recorded in CY 2024, amounting to ₱2.063 million, as an adjustment to prior
period to ensure that the financial statements accurately reflect the
transactions pertaining to CY 2024.

3.11. We recommended and the City Accountant agreed, consistent with the
accrual basis of accounting mandated under Section 350 of RA No. 7160
and Paragraph 7 of IPSAS 1, to refrain from recording PY expenses in
the current year and to record the expenses in the period it occurred.

We recommended that the Accounting Unit prepare adjustments to correct the erroneous accounting entries by Debiting Government Equity and Crediting Prior Period Adjustment to be reflected in the Changes in Net Assets/Equity in CY 2024.
Further, the Accounting Unit should adhere to the prescribed proper recognition or treatment of expense by recognizing it only when the obligation is incurred, and liability should be recorded at the time the goods are received or services rendered pursuant to section 4(s) of the New Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, Volume I and IPSAS 19.
We also recommended that the City Budget Office earmark the amount of PR in the Registry to confirm that it is covered with appropriation but record only the obligation when the city incurs actual commitment.

We recommended that the Accounting Unit recognize on-going land improvement projects under the CIP account and transfer or capitalize the cost of such projects in the appropriate asset account once the project is completed.
We also recommended that the Accounting Unit coordinate with the Engineering Unit by furnishing a copy of the CIP-Land Improvement account schedule so the latter can verify its record and submit a status report of all completed and on-going projects to it.
Accordingly, the Accounting Unit prepare the necessary adjustment of the CIP account to present a reliable balance of the CIP and other PPE affected accounts in the FS.

We recommended that the OIC-CDRRM Officer submit the appropriate supporting documents to prove the distribution of refunded the supplier and officers responsible for the procurement to refund the total amount paid.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor immediately relieve the concerned Special Collecting Officers for violating Section 26 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and Section 15, Chapter 3 of the New Government Accounting System Manual for LGUs Volume 1 due to their unauthorized retention of a huge amount of collections in their possession.
We also recommended that the City Mayor designate a Liquidating Officer with instructions to strictly enforce the regulation on the daily turnover of collections intact and to discharge her functions in accordance with the existing rules.
In addition, we recommended that the City Treasurer conduct and enhance monitoring and oversight mechanisms and, if necessary, conduct an audit or review of collection activities in his office.
In cases of persistent or intentional delays in the remittances of collections, the City Treasurer immediately refer the matter to the City Mayor and recommend the relief of these Accountable Officers.
If warranted, the City Treasurer may recommend that appropriate charges be filed for violating existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Management designate personnel from the GSO and Engineering Unit to review and check the necessity for tire replacement of all motor vehicles and heavy equipment of the City and require them to submit their recommendation for consideration and approval of the City Mayor.
We also recommended that any procurement of tires must be reflected in the PPMP of the requesting end-user to ensure that such program, activities, and projects are planned.
We further recommended that the City Accountant implement a verification process to ensure claims include a Pre-Inspection Report and a WMR with proper approvals and signatures from all relevant personnel.
We therefore recommended that the City fully utilize the GAD fund to address gender-related issues effectively.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to require, the Project In-Charge to closely monitor the implementation of national government-funded projects by conducting a timely evaluation to address the identified causes of delay and ensure that the project is implemented on time in accordance with the agreement.

We recommended that the City, through the City Engineer, require the Contractors to rectify the noted deficiencies otherwise, the equivalent cost of defects/failures will be disallowed in the audit.
We recommended that henceforth, the City Engineer should meticulously inspect all projects and recommend full payment only after final completion of the project and conformance with the plans and specifications.

We recommended and the Management agreed to intensify the collection efforts by resorting to collection remedies like negotiation and compromise settlement otherwise, initiate a request to this Commission an authority to write-off the dormant receivable accounts if warranted.
Likewise, for future loan agreements, the City should carefully screen the capacity to pay of prospective associations and cooperatives to prevent the accumulation of long outstanding receivables.

We reiterate our recommendation that the City Mayor require the City Accountant and the City General Services Officer to reconcile the property records following the guidelines and procedures in COA Circular No. 2020-006 and effect the necessary and adjustments to accurately present the affected accounts in the financial statement.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2023, there is still a discrepancy of ₱7.442 million between the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment (RPCPPE) and the Property, Plant, and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC).

We recommended that Management, through the City Accountant, ensure that henceforth the procurement of multi-purpose vehicles secure the authorization/confirmation from the DILG. Management shall strictly observe the requirements conformably with the existing guidelines on acquiring motor vehicles.

We recommended and the City Assessor agreed to ensure that henceforth the capital gain tax and incidental fees are deducted from the cost of land and that the purchase price will not exceed the land's appraised value. Otherwise, the excess will be disallowed in the audit.

We recommended that the BAC ensure that the prohibited procurement projects during the election period are covered with COMELEC's exception. We also recommended that the City Accountant strictly observe the guidelines on the prohibitions against releases, disbursements, or expenditures of public funds during the election period.

We recommended that Management, through its GAD Focal Person, identify gender issues and include only those programs, activities and projects that will address issues and concerns during the conduct of a gender audit to be funded out of the GAD Fund. We also recommended that programs, projects, and activities not related to address gender issues be separately funded by the General Fund.

We recommended that the City, through its LDRRM Council, review its LDRRM Plan, consolidate all available LDRRM funds, and allocate additional funds to complete the construction of the Evacuation center. We also recommended that the City Engineer implement the construction of the Evacuation Project Phase II based on the timelines in LDRRM Plan so the City could provide enough shelter to its constituents in case of disaster or calamities.
observations.reason: The construction of Multi-purpose Evacuation Center Phase 2 was completed on August 30, 2023 at a contract cost of P5.994 million.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant require the ABC to render a report and accounting of the financial assistance granted to the different barangays. We also recommended that the Management must strictly observe the rules and regulations in the grant, utilization, and liquidation of funds transferred in accordance with the existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management, through the City Accountant, ensure that study tours or related activities have prior authorization from the DILG. We also recommended that Management must strictly observe the requirements conformably with the existing guidelines on study tours or “lakbay aral”.

We recommended that the Budget Office: Observe the proper procedures in preparing the ObR and recording obligations in RAAOs. Obligation through the Obligation Request Form should be filled and taken up once there is already a valid obligation or commitment on the part of the agency. Earmark the obligation in the PR and RAAO to ensure that it has available appropriation before the bidding.

We recommended and the BAC agreed to disseminate RFQs to at least three prospective suppliers or service providers for small value procurements so that the most competitive prices of goods are availed pursuant to the rationale and objectives of R.A. No. 9184. Likewise, we recommended that the City Accountant should strictly impose that RFQs from at least three prospective suppliers are attached to the disbursement voucher before processing the transaction.

We recommended that the City Mayor: Advise the Bids and Awards Committee and concerned personnel in charge of procurement to revisit the procurement process, identify points of possible delay and establish measures to address the flaw to avoid recurrence of delay in the implementation of similar projects; and Direct the BUB Focal Person to closely monitor the implementation of all BUB projects by conducting a timely evaluation to address the identified causes of delay on time.

We recommended that the GSO and Accounting Office maintain relevant records/documents in coordination with the concerned offices for the procurement and issuance of drugs, medicines and other hospital supplies, oil, fuel and lubricants, and other inventory supplies. We also recommended that the City Accountant record the procurement of these supplies as inventory and only take up the corresponding expenses when SSMIs are submitted by the GSO.

We recommended that the City through its LDRRM Council review its LDRRM Plan, consolidate all available LDRRM funds, formulate viable projects and activities, and maximize the use of the funds. We also recommend that the LDDRM Officer ensure the timely utilization of the fund to improve the City’s ability to prepare for and respond to threats of calamities and disasters.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the GSO and Accounting Office maintain relevant records/documents in coordination with the concerned offices for the procurement and issuance of drugs, medicines and other hospital supplies, oil, fuel and lubricants, and other inventory supplies.  These concerned offices should consistently be required to prepare and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to the City Accountant's Office as a basis for recording the cost of the issuances of these items.
We also recommended that the City Accountant record the procurement of these supplies as inventory and only take up the corresponding expenses when SSMIs are submitted by the GSO.

We recommended that the Management require the Accounting Office and GSO to undertake the required reconciliation of property records according to the guidelines and procedures stated in COA Circular No. 2020-006, and effect the necessary adjustments to fairly present the said accounts in the financial statement.

We recommended that Management consider the immediate disposal of unserviceable and obsolete properties. After the disposal, drop from the books of accounts the disposed property and take up any proceeds from the sale thereof, in compliance with paragraph 67 of PAS 16 and section 79 of PD 1445.

We recommended that the City Accountant make a detailed review of the accounts and determine the cause/s of the unreconciled amounts.  Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts.  Henceforth, exercise prudence in recording and classifying the City’s financial transactions to ensure that the balances of the reciprocal accounts are at all times equal.

We recommended that the Management thru the CSWD, LDRRM, and CHO Offices submit the original copies of the distribution lists of goods to the beneficiaries or recipients as a basis for confirmation.  Also, require the Accounting Office to ensure that the itemized list of goods distributed are attached to the DVs for ease of verification.

We recommended that the BAC update or revise their APP and post it on the GPPB online portal.  Also, require the BAC to post the NOA or POs to the PE website and in the GPPB online portal for Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act as required by the GPPB Circular.
We further recommended that the BAC or the Accounting Office furnish the Audit Team the copies of accreditation documents of the supplier-cooperative and ensure that accredited suppliers strictly comply with the requirements of the GPPB Guidelines for Emergency Procurement.

We recommended that the City through its LDRRM Council review its LDRRM Plan, consolidate all available LDRRM funds, formulate viable projects and activities, and maximize the use of available funds.
We also recommend that the LDDRM Officer ensure the timely utilization of the fund to improve the City’s ability to prepare for and respond to threats of calamities and disasters.

We recommended that the Special Collecting Officer remit his collection on time and the City Treasurer ensure that local revenue collectors remit promptly and intact all their collections in consonance with the above-cited regulations.
We recommended that the City maintain a petty cash fund equivalent to their average one-month recurring expenses in consonance with COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended and the Management agreed to inform the Audit Team of any movement of Accountable Officers so that this Office can conduct the mandatory cash examination as required under existing regulations.

We recommended and the Management agreed to intensify the collection efforts by resorting to various collection remedies like negotiation and compromise settlement otherwise, initiate a request to this Commission an authority to write-off the dormant receivable accounts if warranted.
Likewise, for future loan agreements, the City should carefully screen the capacity to pay of prospective associations and cooperatives to prevent the accumulation of long outstanding receivables.

We recommended that the Management ensure that the Offices in-charge of the distribution of construction supplies and materials submit the detailed copies of the distribution lists with recipients’ names and addresses, the itemized supplies and materials they received, and their signatures.  The list should also be summarized showing each item and the total recipients.
Likewise, we recommended that Management direct all concerned officials to support all issuances of construction materials with necessary documents.  Each completed project implemented by the administration must be supported with a duly signed Accomplishment Report, Pre and Post Inspection Report, and photos (before, during, and after) to document the actual repair and maintenance undertaken for each project.

We recommended that:
The Management requires the BAC to disseminate widely the Notice of Bid among qualified suppliers/contractors to obtain competitive prices; and
The BAC periodically reviews the history of bid tenders for particular procurement/project and discern suspicious patterns of bid collusion among suppliers/contractors and impose sanctions thereto if warranted.

We recommended that Management through the BAC ensure that all procurement projects undergo competitive bidding.  The BAC should only resort to alternative methods of procurement in highly exceptional cases or if all the conditions specified thereto are warranted in accordance with Section 48.2 of the R-IRR.

We recommended that the Management through the City Accountant judiciously review the disbursement and impose or deduct from the claim the corresponding liquidated damage, should there be any delay in the delivery of procured supplies and equipment.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Strictly comply with the preparation and accomplishment of Driver’s Trip Tickets and prepare consolidated Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and submit a report for monitoring purposes as provided in the Manual on Audit of Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles, otherwise a Notice of Suspension for these transactions be issued; and
Formulate guidelines to monitor, check and control the issuance of fuel, oil, and lubricants by the gas stations accredited by the City, and the consumption of various offices so that the rules and regulations as provided by law shall be strictly followed.

We recommended that the City through the City Engineer’s Office require the Contractors to rectify the noted deficiencies otherwise the equivalent cost of defects/failures will be disallowed in the audit.
We also recommended that henceforth, the City Engineer inspect meticulously all projects and only recommend full payment if it is implemented according to the project’s plans and specifications.

We recommended that the City Accountant ensure that retention fees should only be released after the lapse of defects liability period of the project in accordance with Sections 62.1 and 62.2 of the R-IRR.

We recommended and the City Treasurer agreed to compute the withholding taxes due on the incentive paid to officers and employees and adjust accordingly their income taxes in the ensuing year.
We also recommended that henceforth, the City Accountant or the City Treasurer ensure that withholding taxes are deducted once incentive benefits paid to officers and employees exceeded the P90,000.00 limit.

We recommended that Management through the City Accountant draw journal entry to correct the affected accounts in the General Fund, as follows:
We also recommended that henceforth, the prescribed journal entries should be observed to record the fund transferred from NGAs and its related transactions in the books for a fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management through the City Budget Office observe the proper procedures in preparing the ObR and recording of obligation in RAAOs. Obligation through the Obligation Request Form should be filled and taken-up once there is already a valid obligation or commitment on the part of the agency.

We recommended that Management require the BAC to disseminate widely the Notice of Bid among qualified suppliers/contractors and consider postponement of bidding if only a single bidder has submitted their bid proposal to promote a competitive bidding process.
Likewise, we recommended that the BAC periodically review the history of bid tenders for particular procurement/project and discern suspicious patterns of bid collusion among suppliers/contractors and impose sanctions thereto if warranted.

We recommended that the Procuring Entity specifically the General Services Office (GSO) and the Bids and Awards Committee ensure that the responded RFQs indicate the brand name of the quoted items and see to it that the PO also specifies the brand names of the items to be procured.

We recommended that Management immediately rescind the service contract with private lawyers and the amount previously paid be refunded for it is contrary to Section 481 of RA No. 7160 and Annex B of the General Principles on Consulting Services under the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that Management ensure to secure prior authority from the SP before disbursement of funds covered by lump-sum appropriation.  Likewise, discontinue the practice of appropriating funds in lump-sum amount, instead, resort to line budgeting to ensure regularity and transparency in government transactions.

We recommended that the City Mayor allocate the unutilized LDRRM Fund after five years to the social services program of the City.
Likewise, we recommended that the City Disaster and Risk Reduction Management Council consider in the formulation of disaster risk reduction management programs/projects/activities the previous year's unexpended balance of LDRRMF to attain maximum utilization of the fund.

We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to draw JEV to correct the affected accounts by debiting Trust Liabilities-DRRMF in the amount of P8,615,385.86, and credit to prior period adjustment as previous year’s income was understated by the same amount.
Henceforth, we also recommended that the prescribed journal entries to record the reversion of unappropriated surplus in the General Fund be observed for a fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the City Accountant ensure that withholding taxes are deducted from payments made under the Revised Withholding Tax Table.  Subsequent to the refund of an overpayment of honoraria, the amount of withholding tax not deducted should be computed for eventual adjustment of income tax to be withheld at year-end.

We recommended that the Management through the City Mayor allocate a Confidential Fund of at least 30% of the agency’s POP budget and charge thereto the expenses related to surveillance or intelligence activities.  Likewise, ensure that the proper procedures in release, utilization, reporting and audit of CF are in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2015-01.

We recommended that Management through the HRMO Officer to strictly comply with the documentary requirements and procedures provided in Sections 22 and 23, Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292 and Civil Service Commission Resolution No. 00034 as regards the monetization of leave credits.

We recommended that Management require the City Treasurer’s Office to forward the complete information of all Stallholders from billing to the collection to Accounting Office for proper recording in the books of accounts and maintenance of Subsidiary Ledgers to support General Ledger account.
We also recommended that the Accounting Office draw appropriate journal entry to record receivables and income in the books of P170,650.00 to fairly present these accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management require the City Accountant to draw journal entry to correct the Accumulated Impairment Losses for - ICT Equipment of P80,197.94, Communication Equipment of P72,182.84, and Other Machinery & Equipment of P948,143.94 by charging it to Prior Period Adjustment.
Henceforth, the prescribed journal entries for derecognition of PPE in the books shall be observed every time there is disposal of assets for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management require the Accounting Office and GSO to undertake the required reconciliation of property records pursuant to Section 114 of Manual on NGAS, Volume I and effect the necessary adjustments to fairly present the said accounts in the financial statement.
We also recommended that the GSO determine ownership of all unrecorded property and equipment found during the physical count, prepare/issue Property Acknowledgement Receipt to the end-user of the item and record the same in the PEC and RPCPPE.  The RPCPPE should include a separate caption of the unrecorded property and equipment found in the station and this shall be the basis of the Accounting Office in recording the subject property and equipment in the books of accounts.
Furthermore, Accounting Office and GSO investigate the noted differences and if found that these properties are missing, hold the accountable officer liable therefor.

In view thereof, it is recommended that the City, specifically the Local School Board to formulate, intensify and expedite the implementation of projects, programs, and activities relative to the operation of public schools in the City.
We recommended the submission of GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2019.
Thus, we recommended that the LGU intensify and expedite the implementation of its LSWMP.

We recommended that the City Accountant observe and see to it that all supporting documents as prescribed by the R-IRR of RA No. 9184, and COA Circular No. 2012-001 are complete and attached to the disbursement vouchers;              
We likewise recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee ensure that the above-mentioned documents are submitted to the City Accountant for attachment to the DV.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that special cash advances should only be granted to duly
designated and bonded special disbursing officers. Elected officials’ cash advance
should only be limited/granted for their official travel expenses pursuant to COA
Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the City’s Bids and Awards Committee to:
a. Strictly abide the standards set forth in the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 by
ensuring that procurement be generally conducted through competitive public
bidding.

b. Stop the practice of splitting government contracts and see to it that the
alternative methods of procurement shall only be resorted in highly exceptional
cases as provided in the pertinent provisions of R A. No. 9184 and its Revised
IRR.

We recommended that:

a. The City Accountant or the agency’s authorized representative to submit to
COA-ATL the DVs covering the cash advances for confidential/intelligence
purposes within seven (7) days after the release of checks as prescribed in
Section 6.1.8.4 of Joint Circular No. 2015-01;

b. The City Accountant to require from the Accountable Officer the required
supporting documents related to P2,310,000.00 cash advances drawn in CY
2017 for confidential/intelligence fund, and to henceforth ensure that all the
required supporting documents enumerated in the checklist of Annex D be
attached in the DV upon submission to COA-ATL;

c. The agency through the City Budget Officer to make sure that the Confidential
Fund will not exceed 30% of the total annual allocation for LGU’s Peace and
Order Program in conformity with the Joint Circular; and

d. Revisit the provisions of Joint Circular No. 2015-01 for guidance in the
disbursement of Confidential Fund.

We recommended that:

a. The City Mayor and CGSO to scrutinize and evaluate carefully the purchase
requests on repair of heavy equipment to ensure the necessity of repair;

b. The City Mayor exercise due care and prudence in the disbursements of funds
to avoid excessive expenditures;

¢. Henceforth, avoid spending government funds without observing the proper
disbursement procedures in compliance with existing laws, rules and
regulations; and

d. The City GSO to exert effort in securing the OR/CR and other pertinent
documents of the heavy equipment to facilitate the immediate insurance of said
property with GSIS pursuant to Section S of RA No. 656.

We recommended that:

a. The City accountant record as inventories small items purchased for disaster
response and rescue activities, which do not qualify under the equipment
classification; and

b. The CGSO issue the covering Inventory Custodian Slip on all items issued to end-
users to establish and determine accountability thereof.

We recommended that the City Mayor thru the HRMO to implement the biometric
timekeeping system or bundy clock to record the personnels attendance in conformity
with existing rules and regulations. Likewise, they should assign a responsible officer
to safeguard the timekeeping machine. These all, to establish an effective control over
the attendance of each employee.

We recommended also that the City Mayor direct the Offices concerned to expedite
the implementation of all projects funded by 20% DF to ensure optimum utilization of
the fund and to assign an Office or personnel to conduct regular monitoring of project
implementation to determine its efficiency.

It is our recommendation that the City should maximize the utilization of the fund so
that the services and benefits intended thereto are fully delivered to the beneficiaries of
the fund.

We recommended to the agency to maximize the utilization of the GAD Fund to fully
address the gender issues at hand.
